quote: One of the cool things about this profession is that you've got the freedom to follow a thread and see where it leads you.
author: David Montgomery
source_outlet: UW College of the Environment podcast

quote: We can restore soil actually fast in decades. It doesn't take centuries. It could be done on policy relevant timescales.
author: David Montgomery
source_outlet: UW College of the Environment podcast

quote: Civilizations that don't take care of their soil don't last.
author: David Montgomery

quote: There's no waste in Nature... everything becomes something else. Circular economy is Nature's economy.
author: David Montgomery

quote: Soil degradation plays out in a time frame way longer than most people pay attention to... it's invisible to the naked eye.
author: David Montgomery

quote: Our rivers should not all look the same. I'd like to celebrate river diversity, and have people think about why a river appears as it does and what processes underlie that appearance.
author: Ellen Wohl

quote: People often think that a messy river, one with downed trees, beaver dams, and all kinds of brush in them are bad, but in fact they are the healthiest kind of river.
author: Ellen Wohl

quote: That's a big component of the fun of research: you start on one path, but never know exactly where it will take you.
author: Ellen Wohl

quote: I'd prefer taking a less controlling approach, where you allow the structure to evolve and don't fasten everything in.
author: Ellen Wohl

quote: Paying attention is a form of reciprocity with the living world, receiving the gifts with open eyes and open heart.
author: Robin Wall Kimmerer

quote: In some Native languages the term for plants translates to 'those who take care of us.'
author: Robin Wall Kimmerer

quote: The currency in a gift economy is relationship, which is expressed as gratitude, as interdependence and the ongoing cycles of reciprocity.
author: Robin Wall Kimmerer

quote: Science polishes the gift of seeing; Indigenous traditions work with gifts of listening and language.
author: Robin Wall Kimmerer

quote: I don't hope for a better climate future. Instead, I'm adamant that I will do whatever I can to build consensuality, to build trust, to build reciprocity with other people.
author: Kyle Whyte

quote: It's not going to take a year, two years, 10 years, 20 — that's the duration of time as measured through kinship.
author: Kyle Whyte

quote: Our sciences are based on the idea that we need to have a good understanding of what it means to be a people who can respond to a constantly changing environment.
author: Kyle Whyte

quote: Recognition, respect, reciprocity, revitalization and renewal are what we all need to be a part of.
author: Nancy Turner

quote: It's really important to develop a partnership, not integrate the knowledge, because they're different knowledge systems, but to listen to people who are living on the land.
author: Nancy Turner

quote: You white people ask too many questions. I can hear Margaret Siwallace say that. Just listen. Just listen.
author: Nancy Turner
source_outlet: YAM Magazine (recounting Nuxalk Elder Margaret Siwallace)

quote: I think that's a responsibility for anyone like myself who has had the privilege of being able to learn this knowledge to make sure that other people understand how important it is.
author: Nancy Turner

quote: Science is a baby knowledge compared to our knowledge of the 12,000 years we've spent here on this land developing our understanding of how we as a people need to interact with each other.
author: Jeannette Armstrong

quote: This water is sacred, nothing on this earth could live without this water no matter how big or small.
author: Jeannette Armstrong

quote: We give our bodies back to the land in a very physical way but we also do other things to the land. We can destroy it, or we can love the land and it can love us back.
author: Jeannette Armstrong

quote: I strive to understand how social-ecological systems can be transformed to be both better and wilder.
author: Kai Chan

quote: Our responsibilities to current and future persons and the natural world call for us all to be social and environmental advocates and activists.
author: Kai Chan

quote: In the jobs-versus-environment debate, neither side is wrong. The problem is the 20th-century economy that forces us to choose.
author: Kai Chan

quote: I find hope in the hope of others.
author: Kai Chan

quote: We get stuck in this place of thinking that it's our responsibility to be perfect, and once we've unlocked that, we should tell other people to do the same.
author: Kai Chan

quote: We are the environment. Whatever we do to the environment, we do directly to ourselves.
author: David Suzuki

quote: We have elevated the economy above the very things that keep us alive. And this is madness.
author: David Suzuki

quote: What I've come to realize through Indigenous people and The Nature of Things is that what is driving us on a destructive path is our anthropocentric way of seeing the world.
author: David Suzuki

quote: We see that we live as one small part of a web of relationships with animals and plants, with air, water, soil, sunlight.
author: David Suzuki

quote: An understanding that everything is connected and what you do has consequences. That's the heart of environmentalism.
author: David Suzuki

quote: When we cut some of the strands, we destroy the integrity of what allows us to be a part of it.
author: David Suzuki

quote: Every language is an old-growth forest of the mind, a watershed, a thought, an ecosystem of spiritual possibilities.
author: Wade Davis

quote: A language is a flash of the human spirit. It's a vehicle through which the soul of each particular culture comes into the material world.
author: Wade Davis

quote: The ethnosphere is humanity's great legacy. It's the symbol of all that we are and all that we can be as an astonishingly inquisitive species.
author: Wade Davis

quote: Every culture is a unique answer to a fundamental question: What does it mean to be human and alive?
author: Wade Davis

quote: The biggest curse of humanity has been cultural myopia, the idea that my world is the real world and everybody else is a failed attempt at being me.
author: Wade Davis

quote: One of the penalties of an ecological education is that one lives alone in a world of wounds.
author: Aldo Leopold

quote: That land is a community is the basic concept of ecology, but that land is to be loved and respected is an extension of ethics.
author: Aldo Leopold

quote: We shall never achieve harmony with land, any more than we shall achieve absolute justice or liberty for people. In these higher aspirations the important thing is not to achieve, but to strive.
author: Aldo Leopold

quote: To keep every cog and wheel is the first precaution of intelligent tinkering.
author: Aldo Leopold
source_outlet: A Sand County Almanac, 'The Round River', p. 190

quote: Conservation is a state of harmony between men and land. Harmony with land is like harmony with a friend; you cannot cherish his right hand and chop off his left.
author: Aldo Leopold
source_outlet: A Sand County Almanac, 'The Ecological Conscience'

quote: Only the mountain has lived long enough to listen objectively to the howl of a wolf.
author: Aldo Leopold
source_outlet: A Sand County Almanac, 'Thinking Like a Mountain'

quote: Kindly use depends upon intimate knowledge, the most sensitive responsiveness and responsibility.
author: Wendell Berry

quote: It is impossible to care for each other more or differently than we care for the earth.
author: Wendell Berry

quote: Do unto those downstream as you would have those upstream do unto you.
author: Wendell Berry

quote: We have lived by the assumption that what was good for us would be good for the world. We have been wrong. We must change our lives, so that it will be possible to live by the contrary assumption that what is good for the world will be good for us.
author: Wendell Berry

quote: I see that the life of this place is always emerging beyond expectation or prediction or typicality, that it is unique, given to the world minute by minute, only once, never to be repeated. And this is when I see that this life is a miracle, absolutely worth having, absolutely worth saving.
author: Wendell Berry

quote: It is easy for me to imagine that the next great division of the world will be between people who wish to live as creatures and people who wish to live as machines.
author: Wendell Berry

quote: A community is the mental and spiritual condition of knowing that the place is shared.
author: Wendell Berry
source_outlet: The Long-Legged House (1969), 'The Loss of the Future'
